title: Meghan O'Sullivan on Office Hours Podcast
summary: Meghan O’Sullivan, Jeane Kirkpatrick Professor of the Practice of International Affairs at the Harvard Kennedy School and author of 'Windfall: How the New Energy Abundance Upends Global Politics and Strengthens America’s Power,' talks with Aroop Mukharji (@aroopmukharji) about fracking, America’s strategic petroleum reserve, energy abundance, and her early start in geopolitics.
body: Meghan L. O’Sullivan is the Jeane Kirkpatrick Professor of the Practice of International Affairs and Director of the Geopolitics of Energy Project at Harvard University’s Kennedy School. Her expertise includes the geopolitics of energy, decision making in foreign policy, nation-building, counterinsurgency, and the Middle East. _list.source: Belfer Center for Science and International Affairs, Harvard Kennedy School

result.main_content_en: The article features Meghan L. O'Sullivan, a professor at Harvard Kennedy School, discussing energy geopolitics on a podcast. Her expertise spans the geopolitics of energy, foreign policy decision-making, nation-building, counterinsurgency, and the Middle East. The context includes related reports on great power competition involving climate, trade, and U.S.-China-EU relations, as well as methane abatement costs in the oil and gas industry. The discussion touches on the impact of potential future U.S. political shifts on climate policy. The content connects energy markets, geopolitical strategy, and environmental policy, highlighting the intersection of resource economics, international relations, and climate change mitigation efforts within a framework of global rivalry and cooperation.
result.main_content_zh: 本文介绍了哈佛大学肯尼迪学院教授梅根·L·奥沙利文在一档播客节目中探讨能源地缘政治。她的专业领域涵盖能源地缘政治、外交政策决策、国家建设、反叛乱和中东问题。相关内容涉及大国竞争背景下的气候、贸易和美中欧关系报告，以及油气行业甲烷减排成本分析。讨论还涉及美国未来潜在政治变化对气候政策的影响。内容将能源市场、地缘政治战略和环境政策联系起来，突出了在全球竞争与合作框架下，资源经济学、国际关系和气候变化减缓工作的交叉点。
